name: Rolled Sugar Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together the margarine, sugar, vanilla, and egg until light and fluffy.
2. Add about three-quarters of the flour and baking powder to the mixture; reserve the rest for rolling out.
3. Blend everything until well mixed.
4. Chill the dough for 2 hours or overnight.
5. Roll out on a lightly floured surface until 1/8 inch thick.
6. Cut with a cookie cutter.
7. Place cookies on an ungreased baking sheet.
8. Bake in a 375°F oven until lightly browned, about 5-10 minutes.
9. Cool before storing.
Makes about 72 cookies (2 inches) or 6 small cookie sheets of various sizes.
My note: If you aren't too fussy, square or random shapes from the edges can be cut with a plastic knife or anything safe to use on your rolling surface. Decorate with a tiny piece of cherry, raisin, or nut.
Two small cookies = 58 calories, 1/2 bread exchange, 1/2 fat exchange; 8 grams carbohydrate, 0 protein, 2 fat, 46 mg sodium, 10 potassium, 8 mg cholesterol.
Source: Am. Diabetes Assoc. Holiday Cookbook by Betty Wedman, 1986
Shared and tested by Elizabeth Rodier Nov 93.
